lesson.menu_desc: The Subjunctive in Formal Recommendations (It is essential that + base verb) -- Communicating Crises to Leadership -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 17 -- Disruption Management -- The Subjunctive (It is essential that the team be notified)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Fabiana Michelly Silva | Aula 17 -- Disruption Management

listenings.text: Good morning. I am briefing you on a supply-chain incident that began overnight. At about two a.m. we had a system outage at the main distribution hub, followed by a data breach in the supplier portal. The severity is high, so I recommend that we treat this as a priority-one event. Here is where we stand. Containment is in progress, and the team has applied a stopgap that keeps orders flowing while we work on a permanent fix. It is essential that leadership be informed before the market opens, and it is critical that the affected suppliers be notified within the hour. I would ask that each work stream provide a short status by ten o'clock. For damage control, I propose that no one comment publicly until our statement is ready. Once the outage is fully contained, we will run a postmortem to find the root issue and publish a recovery plan. I recommend that we flag up any further failure to me directly. Thank you, and I will take questions now.

Hi Marcus, this is Fabiana with an urgent update on the incident. The outage is now contained, and the stopgap is holding, so orders are still moving. The breach was narrower than we feared, but the severity is still high, so it is essential that we keep leadership briefed every two hours. I have already notified the three affected suppliers, and I asked that each one provide a written recovery plan by end of day. On the fallout side, I recommend that we prepare a short statement now, before any news leaks, so damage control is ahead of the story rather than behind it. I propose that we schedule the postmortem for tomorrow morning while the details are fresh, and I insist that the on-call engineer be included so we capture the real root issue. One more thing: I will flag up anything new the moment it appears. Please call me back when you can. Thanks, and talk soon.

extra_audio.text: Overnight, a system outage at the distribution hub triggers a data breach in the supplier portal. Marcus rates the severity as high and recommends that the incident be treated as priority one. The team applies a stopgap so orders keep flowing while containment is in progress. Marcus insists that leadership be briefed and that the affected suppliers be notified within the hour. Once the outage is contained, Fabiana asks that each supplier provide a recovery plan and proposes that a postmortem be scheduled to find the root issue.
